page: Google Cloud Platform c4-standard-96 is a compute-focused server instance powered by an Intel Xeon 8581C processor utilizing the x86_64 architecture. It features 96 virtual CPUs mapped to 48 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled, operating at a base clock speed of 2.3 GHz. The system is equipped with 360 GB of dedicated system memory, yielding a resource density of 3.75 GB of RAM per vCPU. The processor architecture integrates 260 MB of L3 cache and supports advanced instruction sets, including Intel Advanced Vector Extensions (AVX-512) and Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX) for deep learning and matrix operations. This instance does not include local storage or bundled GPU hardware.

Benchmark evaluations indicate strong multi-core processing capabilities, with the instance achieving top-tier performance in Geekbench single-core and multi-core metrics, as well as Passmark CPU tests. The hardware demonstrates high efficiency in text processing, image processing, and multi-threaded bzip3 decompression, all placing in the top 10% of tested systems. Memory bandwidth tests for both cached small blocks and uncached large blocks show strong read and write performance, although the overall Passmark memory score falls within the average range. For specialized workloads, the server delivers top-tier throughput in static web serving and small-model LLM prompt processing, alongside strong performance in medium-model LLM inference and database operations.

From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the c4-standard-96 offers high compute density and advanced instruction support, making it highly effective for parallel processing tasks without the overhead of dedicated GPU hardware. However, the lack of local NVMe storage requires reliance on network-attached storage, which may introduce I/O latency tradeoffs for storage-heavy applications. The instance is highly suited for compute-intensive workloads such as software compilation, static web serving, text and image processing, and CPU-based machine learning inference.
description: Google Cloud Platform c4-standard-96 is an x86_64 compute-focused server instance featuring 96 vCPUs, 48 physical cores, and 360 GB of RAM, powered by an Intel Xeon 8581C processor running at 2.3 GHz. It includes 260 MB of L3 cache and supports Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX) and AVX-512. The instance lacks local storage and GPU accelerators. In benchmarks, the c4-standard-96 delivers top-tier performance in multi-core processing, static web serving, and text processing, alongside strong memory bandwidth and database operation throughput. Qualitatively, it provides high compute density for parallel workloads, though the absence of local storage represents a tradeoff for I/O-heavy tasks. It is highly suited for software compilation, CPU-based LLM inference, and high-throughput web serving.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform c4-standard-96 is a compute-focused instance with 96 vCPUs, 360 GB RAM, and an Intel Xeon 8581C CPU, delivering top-tier multi-core and text processing performance.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform c4-standard-96 is an x86_64 compute instance with 96 vCPUs, 360 GB RAM, and top-tier multi-core benchmark performance.
tagline: A high-density compute instance featuring advanced matrix extensions and strong multi-core performance for parallel processing and web serving workloads.
bullet_points: 96 vCPUs and 360 GB RAM powered by an Intel Xeon 8581C processor.
Intel AMX and AVX-512 support for accelerated matrix and vector operations.
Top-tier benchmark performance in multi-core processing and static web serving.
No local storage or GPU accelerators included in the hardware profile.
Best suited for software compilation, CPU-based inference, and database operations.
